The results demonstrate MLPs potential for short-term hydrological forecasting while suggesting future improvements with physicsinformed neural networks (PINNs).

titulo: Evaluating 2024 Brazilian wildfires using the ST-DBSCAN clustering approach
abstract: Wildfires represent one of the main environmental pressures in Brazil, linked to agricultural expansion and climate variability. This study investigates the spatio-temporal distribution of fires in Brazil between May and September 2024, using the ST-DBSCAN algorithm on fire outbreak data from INPE. The analysis revealed that the the more outbreaks the clusters contained, the higher the FRP were. The spatial analysis indicated a strong association between anthropogenic use and cover matrices for the Amazon and the Atlantic Forest, while for Pantanal, Cerrado and Caatinga, bigger clusters were more associated with natural classes. Pampa shown a mixed pattern between natural and anthropogenic uses, without clusters with more than 101 outbreaks.

titulo: Qualitative Analysis of the 2022 Census Geolocation
abstract: Geolocation plays a crucial role in the analysis of geographic data, assisting businesses and governments in making more precise decisions. To ensure the reliability of this information, it is essential that the coordinates exhibit high accuracy. This study compares two databases in Belo Horizonte, the CNEFE and an address database from Prodabel, to analyze the quality of geolocation data captured in the 2022 census. To achieve this, we conducted a manual comparative analysis of discrepant values for the same address, validated the information using Google Street View, and sought to evaluate the quality of the captured coordinates. The aim is to highlight the importance of high-quality geographic data for the socioeconomic development of the country.

titulo: Enhancing Change Detection in SAR Image Time Series through Image Denoising Algorithms
abstract: This study builds upon the Energy Correlation Screening (ECS) technique to identify spatio-temporal changes in time series of SAR imagery. To reduce the speckle noise inherent in radar data, several image restoration methods are examined in conjunction with this methodology to enhance its performance. After careful evaluation, the Bilateral filter demonstrated the most significant contribution to the model compared to all other algorithms tested.

titulo: GeoSaudeBrasil: Banco de Dados Geoespacial de Crescimento Populacional e Leitos Hospitalares no Brasil para Anï؟½lises Temporais (2007-2024) e Regionais em Diferentes Escalas Geograficas
abstract: A distribuicï؟½ao desigual da infraestrutura hospitalar no Brasil im-  pacta diretamente o acesso aos servicï؟½os de saude, especialmente em regi ï؟½ oes  remotas e vulneraveis. Embora existam dados p ï؟½ ublicos sobre leitos hospita- ï؟½ lares e populacï؟½ao, eles encontram-se dispersos em diferentes fontes e forma-  tos, dificultando analises espacï؟½o-temporais integradas. A fragmentacï؟½ ï؟½ ao dos  dados publicos sobre infraestrutura hospitalar e crescimento populacional no ï؟½ Brasil dificulta a realizacï؟½ao de an  alises espacï؟½o-temporais robustas, al ï؟½ em de ï؟½ demandar esforcï؟½o significativo de coleta e integracï؟½ao por parte dos pesqui-  sadores. Este estudo tem como objetivo apresentar o GeoSaudeBrasil, um banco de dados geoespacial estruturado e normalizado que consolida registros do MINISTERIO DA SA ï؟½ UDE DO BRASIL - DATASUS e projecï؟½ ï؟½ oes popu- lacionais do IBGE para o perï؟½ؤ±odo de 2007 a 2024. Implementado em PostgreSQL com extensao PostGIS, o banco consolida dados previamente dispersos  e permite a realizacï؟½ao de an  alises espaciais e temporais em diferentes escalas ï؟½ geograficas. A modelagem relacional adotada possibilita a geracï؟½ ï؟½ ao de indi-  cadores espacï؟½otemporais, apoiando estudos sobre desigualdades regionais no acesso a sa ` ude. Para ilustrar a aplicabilidade do banco, foi desenvolvido um ï؟½ estudo de caso exploratorio focado na an ï؟½ alise da oferta de leitos na regi ï؟½ ao  Centro-Oeste de Minas Gerais. O banco de dados esta dispon ï؟½ ï؟½ؤ±vel publicamente em https://ufsj.edu.br/marconi/geosaudebrasil.php. ABSTRACT: The uneven distribution of hospital infrastructure in Brazil directly impacts access to health services, especially in remote and vulnerable regions. Although public data on hospital beds and population are available, they are dispersed across different sources and formats, making integrated spatiotemporal analyses challenging. This data fragmentation hinders the execution of robust spatiotemporal studies and demands significant efforts from researchers to collect and integrate information. This study aims to present GeoSaudeBrasil, a structured and normalized geospatial database that consolidates records from the MINISTERIO DA SA ï؟½ UDE DO BRASIL - DATASUS and ï؟½ population projections from IBGE for the period from 2007 to 2024. Implemented in PostgreSQL with PostGIS extension, the database unifies previously scattered datasets and enables spatial and temporal analyses at multiple geographic scales. The adopted relational modeling supports the generation of spatiotemporal indicators, assisting studies on regional inequalities in access to healthcare services. To illustrate the databases applicability, an exploratory case study focusing on hospital bed availability in the Central-West region of Minas Gerais was conducted. The database is publicly available at https://ufsj.edu.br/marconi/geosaudebrasil.php.

titulo: StreetNets - A tool for network analysis on street maps
abstract: This paper presents StreetNets, a tool for analyzing urban street networks using OpenStreetMap data. It supports three analyses: descriptive statistics of street networks, betweenness centrality for identifying critical streets, and correlation with geospatial data with an example of flood patterns. A case study of Brazils top 50 GDP cities explores key network metrics, while betweenness centrality identifies important streets in major cities. The tool integrates flood data with Sao Paulos street network, offering an automated and intuitive  platform for urban analysis using OSMNx, GeoPandas, and the visualization libraries matplotlib and plotly.

titulo: Identificaï؟½ï؟½o de fatores determinantes para o cultivo de cacau por meio de regressï؟½o espacial no Projeto de Assentamento Itatï؟½, Parï؟½
abstract: O cultivo de cacau, relevante para a economia do Parï؟½, se expande no sudoeste do estado, onde se localiza o Projeto de Assentamento Itatï؟½. O mapeamento dessa cultura e fundamental para compreender sua distribuiï؟½ï؟½o  e os fatores espaciais associados a sua ocorrï؟½ncia. Este estudo avaliou a influï؟½ncia de variï؟½veis indicadoras do cultivo de cacau por meio de regressï؟½o  espacial, comparando um modelo Conditional Autoregressive Model e um modelo Geographically Weighted Regression. Foram analisadas cinco variï؟½veis independentes derivadas de um estudo previo com a tï؟½cnica AHP. Os resultados indicam que o GWR apresentou maior capacidade de captar padrï؟½es espaciais,  destacando-se as variï؟½veis 'influï؟½ncia do uso e ocupaï؟½ï؟½o da terra' e distï؟½ncia  ï؟½s estradas na distribuiï؟½ï؟½o do cultivo. ABSTRACT: Cocoa cultivation, which is important to the economy of Parï؟½, is expanding in the southwest of the state, where the Itata Settlement Project is located. Mapping this crop is essential to understand its distribution and the spatial factors associated with its occurrence. This study evaluated the influence of variables indicating cocoa cultivation through spatial regression, comparing a Conditional Autoregressive Model and a Geographically Weighted Regression model. Five independent variables derived from a previous study using the AHP technique were analyzed. The results indicate that the GWR presented a greater capacity to capture spatial patterns, with emphasis on the variables influence of land use and occupation and distance to roads in the distribution of the crop.
